description, URL, and dates.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__read_teams_message
function.description:
Read a single Teams chat message by chat ID and message ID.

Use this after search_teams_messages or poll_teams_messages to get full
message detail including mentions, attachments, threading, and edit history.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__reply_to_email
function.description:
Reply to an existing email (or reply-all) and send immediately.

When ``content_type`` is ``"HTML"`` (the default) the reply is
produced the same way the Outlook client does — the user's HTML is
placed above the auto-quoted original thread. With
``content_type="Text"`` Graph handles the reply directly using the
plain-text ``comment`` field (also auto-quoted).

body.description: Reply body content (HTML or plain text per content_type).

message_id.description: Graph message id of the email being replied to.

reply_all.description: If True, reply to every recipient on the original.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__resolve_sharepoint_url
function.description:
Resolve a SharePoint or OneDrive URL to Graph API identifiers.

Accepts any browser-copyable SharePoint or OneDrive URL and returns
the drive_id and item_id needed for other SharePoint tools.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__respond_to_calendar_event
function.description:
Accept, tentatively accept, or decline an event invite.

Note: ``response="tentative"`` hits Graph's ``tentativelyAccept``
action internally.

comment.description: Optional comment sent to the organizer.

send_response.description:
If True, the organizer receives the response.
If False, the RSVP is recorded silently.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__search_calendar_events
function.description:
Search calendar events with flexible filters.

All filters can be freely combined (AND logic). Text matching is
case-insensitive substring against subject, body preview, and location.

Use query='*' or omit to browse all events in the date range.
When combining broad queries with tight filters, results may be
fewer than limit due to client-side filtering.

after_datetime.description: Events starting after this date (ISO 8601). Default: 30 days ago.

attendee.description: Filter by attendee email (partial match, any TO/CC attendee).

before_datetime.description: Events ending before this date (ISO 8601). Default: 30 days from now.

calendar_name.description: Restrict search to a specific calendar by exact name (e.g. "Personal", "Work").

check_transcripts.description:
If True, check each Teams meeting for transcripts
(2 extra API calls per unique meeting). Default: False.

compact.description:
If True, return a token-efficient minimal shape per event
(id, subject, start, end, duration, organizer_name, attendee_names,
is_all_day, is_cancelled, has_external_attendees). Strips body_preview,
join_url, web_link, full attendee objects, categories, sensitivity, etc.
Default: False.

exclude_cancelled.description: If True, filter out events where isCancelled is True. Default: False.

offset.description: Results to skip for pagination (default 0, max 5000).

query.description: Text to search in subject, body, or location. Use '*' or omit for all events.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__search_emails
function.description:
Search emails in the authenticated user's mailbox.

All filters can be freely combined. Text search params (sender,
recipient, subject, query) use Microsoft Graph KQL search. When
text search is active, structured filters (dates, is_read,
importance) are applied client-side after fetching results.

When combining broad text searches with tight date filters,
results may be fewer than ``limit`` because client-side filtering
trims the over-fetched set.

folder.description: Folder name to search in (e.g. "Inbox", "Sent Items").

has_attachments.description:
Filter by attachment presence. Note: Graph API
does not count inline images as attachments, so emails with
only inline images will have has_attachments=False.

offset.description:
Number of results to skip for pagination (default 0,
max 5000). Use with limit to page through results.

query.description: Free-text search across subject, body, and attachments.

recipient.description: Filter by recipient email (matches TO, CC, or BCC).

sender.description: Filter by sender email address (full or partial).

subject.description: Filter where subject contains this string.

subject_exact.description: Filter where subject exactly matches this string.

subject_startswith.description: Filter where subject starts with this string.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__search_meetings_by_name
function.description: Search for meetings by name/subject using case-insensitive substring matching.

check_transcripts.description: Optional. If True, include has_transcript field. Default: False.

search_term.description: The search term to match against meeting subjects.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__search_sharepoint_pages
function.description:
Search SharePoint site pages by filename, author, or date.

Lists pages on a SharePoint site with server-side filtering.
Pass any URL on the target site — the tool extracts the site
automatically (no resolve_sharepoint_url call needed).

All filters are server-side to minimise token usage. Use
name_contains for keyword search (matches the .aspx filename,
which is a hyphenated version of the page title). Use
name_range_start/end for alphabetical paging on large sites.

**Recommended workflow:** Search with metadata only (default),
hand the user ``web_url`` links. Use ``include_content="text"``
for lightweight content preview. Use ``read_sharepoint_page``
with the returned ``site_id`` and page ``id`` for full content.

author.description:
Filter by last modified author. Full name uses
exact match; single word uses partial match.

include_content.description:
"none" (default) returns metadata only.
"text" returns page content as plain text (lightweight).
"html" returns raw HTML from canvasLayout.

name_prefix.description:
Prefix match on filename (e.g. "Ho" matches
"Holiday-Calenders.aspx" and "Home.aspx").

name_range_end.description:
Alphabetical upper bound (e.g. "F" for
pages before F). Use "Z{" for all through Z.

name_range_start.description:
Alphabetical lower bound (e.g. "A" for
pages starting A onwards). Combine with name_range_end.

site_url.description:
Any URL on the target SharePoint site (e.g.
"https://waterfield365.sharepoint.com/sites/Intranet").

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__search_sharepoint_sites
function.description:
Search SharePoint sites the authenticated user can access.

Returns sites matching a keyword. Use ``query="*"`` (the default)
to list all sites the user has permission to access. This is the
recommended first step when the user asks about SharePoint content
and you don't know which site to target.

The returned ``id`` can be passed to ``read_sharepoint_site`` for
full metadata, or to ``read_sharepoint_page`` / ``search_sharepoint_pages``
(via site_url from ``web_url``) to work with site pages.

query.description:
Search keyword to match site name or content.
Use "*" (default) to list all accessible sites.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__search_teams_messages
function.description: Search Teams chat messages across all chats using keyword or KQL syntax.

after_datetime.description: ISO 8601 — only messages on or after this date.

before_datetime.description: ISO 8601 — only messages on or before this date.

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default 0).

query.description: Free-text or KQL query. Supports from:, sent:, hasAttachment: operators.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__send_email
function.description: Send an email as the authenticated user.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__send_email_on_behalf
function.description:
Send an email from a shared or delegated mailbox.

Hits Graph's ``/users/{upn}/sendMail`` endpoint. Requires the Entra
app registration to have the delegated ``Mail.Send.Shared`` scope
pre-consented AND the authenticated user to have Send As / Send on
Behalf rights on ``mailbox_upn``. If either is missing Graph returns
HTTP 403 — the admin must grant the scope and/or mailbox permission.

mailbox_upn.description:
UPN / email of the sending mailbox (e.g.
"shared@waterfield.com").

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__send_teams_message
function.description: Send a text message to a Teams chat.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__send_teams_message_and_wait
function.description:
Create a chat, send a message, and poll until a reply arrives.

Orchestrates the full send-and-wait workflow:
1. Create chat with members
2. Send the message
3. Poll for new messages until a reply or timeout

max_wait_seconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait for a reply (default 600).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__sharepoint_folder_search
function.description:
Search for folders across SharePoint and OneDrive by name.

the authenticated user has access to.

query.description: Folder name to search for (partial match).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__sharepoint_search
function.description:
Search SharePoint and OneDrive documents by content.

Searches across all SharePoint sites and OneDrive files the
authenticated user has access to. Returns matching documents
with metadata and content snippets.

All filters can be freely combined to narrow results.

When fallback=False (default), performs a single fast search and
returns a flat list of results. When fallback=True, automatically
retries with alternative query strategies if the primary search
returns no results (lowercase, wildcard, first-two-words), and
includes disambiguation info for duplicate file names.

after_date.description:
Only include documents modified after this date
(ISO 8601, e.g. "2026-01-01").

author.description: Filter by author name or email (partial match).

before_date.description:
Only include documents modified before this date
(ISO 8601, e.g. "2026-03-01").

fallback.description:
Enable intelligent fallback strategies when the
primary search returns no results (default False). When
True, retries with lowercase, wildcard, and first-two-words
queries, and returns disambiguation info. Uses 1-4 API
calls instead of 1.

folder_name.description: Filter by parent folder name (partial match).

max_size_mb.description:
Only include files at most this size in MB
(e.g. 10.0 for files <= 10 MB). Applied client-side, so
fewer results than limit may be returned.

min_size_mb.description:
Only include files at least this size in MB
(e.g. 5.0 for files >= 5 MB). Applied client-side, so
fewer results than limit may be returned.

offset.description:
Number of results to skip for pagination (default 0,
max 1000).

query.description:
Free-text search query. Searches document content,
filenames, and metadata.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__sharepoint_url_to_path
function.description:
Parse a SharePoint URL into site, folder path, and file name.

Performs local URL parsing only — no API call required. Useful for
quickly extracting path components from SharePoint URLs.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-m365-staging__update_calendar_event
function.description:
Partially update a calendar event. Only provided fields are changed.

Series vs single occurrence is determined by the ``event_id`` the
caller passes: a series-master id updates the whole series; an
instance id (from calendarView or the ``instances`` endpoint) edits
just that occurrence.

``attendees=None`` leaves the attendee list unchanged; passing
``attendees=[]`` explicitly clears it.

attendees.description: New attendee list (see create_calendar_event for shape).

event_id.description: Graph calendar event id (series master or occurrence).

is_online_meeting.description:
Set to True to provision a new Teams meeting
on an event that does not yet have one. Setting to False on
an event that already has a Teams meeting raises
``ValueError`` — Graph's PATCH endpoint silently ignores
that field combination, so the service refuses rather than
return a misleading success. To remove a Teams meeting from
an existing event, delete and recreate it without
``is_online_meeting``.

timezone.description: Applied to start/end when either is provided.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-pnc-staging__authenticate
function.description: The `claude.ai a1b-mcp-pnc-staging` MCP server (claudeai-proxy at https://a1b-mcp-pnc-staging-dsgqbdb0d8hkhjcw.z03.azurefd.net/mcp) is installed but requires authentication. Call this tool to start the OAuth flow — you'll receive an authorization URL to share with the user. Once the user completes authorization in their browser, the server's real tools will become available automatically.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-pnc-staging__complete_authentication
function.description: Complete an in-progress OAuth flow for the `claude.ai a1b-mcp-pnc-staging` MCP server by submitting the callback URL. Call `mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-pnc-staging__authenticate` first to start the flow and get the authorization URL. After the user authorizes in their browser, the browser is redirected to a `http://localhost:<port>/callback?code=...&state=...` URL — on remote sessions that page fails to load, but the URL in the address bar is still valid. Pass that full URL here as `callback_url`.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-salesops-staging__modify_booking_session
function.description:
Server-side orchestration for booking workflow lifecycle.

The agent provides a mode and payload; the server executes all
Cosmos session updates, ticket patches, and verifications
internally with a single consistent timestamp.

comment.description: Procedural progress description. No PII or financials.

mode.description: One of "claim", "step", or "end".

output_refs.description: (end) JSON array of {"name", "ref"} for existing docs to link.

owner_name.description: (claim) Display name of who is claiming.

runstep_refs.description: (step) Optional JSON array of runstep ref IDs.

step_id.description: (step) Step number to mark as completed.

store_files.description: (step/end) Optional JSON array of {"name", "content"} to store in protected-data. In step mode, creates a runstep per step and adds refs to the step's run. In end mode, skips files already uploaded by previous steps.

workflow_steps.description: (claim) JSON array of step names, e.g. '["Data Gathering", "Analysis", "GUI Report", "Cleanup"]'.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-salesops-staging__ocr_process_batch
function.description:
OCR multiple SharePoint documents in a single batch.

Submits all documents concurrently to Azure Document Intelligence and
tracks them under a single batch ID. Each document is processed in the
background. Use ocr_check_batch to poll for completion.

output_format.description: Output format for all documents — "md" (default) or "html".

sharepoint_urls.description:
JSON array of SharePoint file URLs, or a single URL string.
Example: '["https://sp.com/contract.pdf", "https://sp.com/quote.pdf"]'

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_a1b-mcp-salesops-staging__ocr_process_document
function.description:
OCR a SharePoint document and upload the result back to the same folder.

Downloads the file from SharePoint, sends it to Azure Document Intelligence
for OCR, and uploads the extracted text (markdown or HTML) back to the
original SharePoint folder.

output_format.description: Output format — "md" (default) or "html".

sharepoint_url.description:
Direct SharePoint file URL pointing to the document.
Must be a clean file path URL, e.g.:
"https://contoso.sharepoint.com/Shared Documents/Invoices/scan.pdf"
or "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Sales/Shared Documents/report.pdf".
Do NOT pass a SharePoint list view URL (containing AllItems.aspx or
query parameters like viewid=). If you only have a list view URL,
extract the file path from the "id" query parameter first.
Sharing links like "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/:b:/s/Sales/EfG1h2..."
are also accepted.

wait_for_processing.description:
If True, wait for OCR to complete and return
the result URL. If False (default), start processing in the
background and return a job ID for polling.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_D365__dataverse_update
function.description:
Update an account, opportunity, or project record in Dynamics 365.

Performs a PATCH operation on the specified record with the provided fields.
Field names use friendly aliases — unknown fields are rejected with a warning.
Lookup fields accept email, name, or GUID and are resolved automatically.

fields.description:
JSON string of field names and values to update.

**Account fields:**
  Text: name, description, phone, phone2, phone3, fax, email, email2,
    website, account_number, sic, ticker, stock_exchange,
    address1_name/line1/line2/city/state/zip/country,
    address2_name/line1/line2/city/state/zip/country
  Money: revenue, credit_limit
  Numeric: employees
  Choice (int codes): industry_code, customer_type_code,
    account_category_code, account_rating_code, ownership_code,
    payment_terms_code
  Boolean: credit_on_hold, do_not_email, do_not_phone, do_not_fax,
    do_not_postal_mail
  Lookups: owner (email), primary_contact (email/name),
    parent_account (name/GUID), currency (ISO/name/GUID)
  Clear: clear_primary_contact, clear_parent_account

**Opportunity fields:**
  Text: name, description, email, current_situation, customer_need,
    proposed_solution, step_name
  Date: estimated_close_date, actual_close_date, final_decision_date
  Money: estimated_value, budget_amount, freight_amount
  Choice (int codes): rating_code (1=Hot,2=Warm,3=Cold), priority_code,
    order_type, forecast_category, sales_stage_code, budget_status,
    purchase_process, purchase_timeframe
  Boolean: is_revenue_system_calculated
  Lookups: owner (email), account_manager (email), account (name/GUID),
    contact (email/name/GUID), price_list (name/GUID),
    contracting_unit (name/GUID), currency (ISO/name/GUID)
  Clear: clear_contact, clear_account_manager

**Project fields:**
  Text: name, description, comments, value_statement
  Date: scheduled_start, finish, actual_start, actual_end
  Numeric: hours_per_day (float), hours_per_week (float),
    days_per_month (int)
  Choice (int codes): overall_status, schedule_mode,
    bulk_generation_status
  Boolean: allow_percent_complete,
    disable_team_member_auto_create
  Lookups: project_manager (email), owner (email),
    customer (name/GUID), contracting_unit (name/GUID)
  Clear: clear_project_manager

identifier.description:
Record identifier:
Account:      account number (A.XXXXXXX) or GUID
Opportunity:  opportunity number (O.XXXXXXX) or GUID
Project:      project number (P.XXXXXX), contract ID (ORD-...), or GUID

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_D365__dataverse_update_activity
function.description:
Update a timeline item (note, task, phone call, or email) by GUID.

Performs a PATCH operation on the specified activity or annotation record.

fields.description:
JSON string of Dataverse field names and values to update.
Examples:
- Note: '{"subject": "New Title", "notetext": "Updated body"}'
- Task: '{"percentcomplete": 75, "scheduledend": "2026-04-01"}'
- Phone call: '{"description": "Updated notes", "directioncode": true}'
- Email: '{"subject": "Revised subject"}'

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_D365__list_accounts
function.description:
List Dynamics 365 accounts where a person is the owner.

Searches accounts by owner name (substring match on the Owner
field). Supports pagination.

Intended for account executives, customer experience architects,
and sales managers to quickly review their account portfolio.

email.description:
Owner's email — converted to name automatically
   (e.g. "dan.topp@waterfield.com" -> "Dan Topp").
   Optional — defaults to the logged-in user from the auth token.

name.description:
Owner's display name (substring match, e.g. "Rick Jones").
   Optional — defaults to the logged-in user from the auth token.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_D365__list_opportunities
function.description:
List Dynamics 365 opportunities — person-scoped or org-wide.

Unified tool for opportunity pipeline queries. Supports:
- **Flat list**: person's opportunities with role/date/status filters.
- **Stage counts**: `list_stages=True` returns pipeline stages with counts.
- **Stage drill-down**: `stage="Procurement"` returns opportunities in that stage.
- **Org-wide**: `scope="org"` for the entire pipeline (server-side aggregation).

after.description: Only opportunities with start date >= this value (YYYY-MM-DD).

before.description: Only opportunities with end date <= this value (YYYY-MM-DD).

email.description: Person's email — auto-converted to name. Ignored when scope="org".

list_stages.description: If True, return stage counts instead of records.

name.description:
Person's display name (substring match, e.g. "Rick Jones").
   Defaults to logged-in user. Ignored when scope="org".

next_link.description: Cursor from previous page (org-wide drill-down only).

role.description:
Filter by role involvement (person-scoped only). One of:
- "owner", "presales", "csm", "salesops", "all" (default)

stage.description:
Pipeline stage to drill into (e.g. "Procurement", "Won-Review").
   Ignored if list_stages=True.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_D365__list_projects
function.description:
List Dynamics 365 projects — person-scoped or org-wide.

Unified tool for project queries. Supports:
- **Flat list**: person's projects with role/date/status filters.
- **Status counts**: `list_statuses=True` returns counts by status dimensions.
- **Status drill-down**: `drill_into="In Progress"` returns projects in that status.
- **Org-wide**: `scope="org"` for counts across all projects (server-side aggregation).

after.description: Only projects with start >= this value (YYYY-MM-DD). Flat list only.

before.description: Only projects with finish <= this value (YYYY-MM-DD). Flat list only.

drill_into.description: Value to drill into from any dimension (e.g. "In Progress", "Active", "At Risk").

email.description: Person's email — auto-converted to name. Ignored when scope="org".

group_by.description:
Dimensions for counting (comma-separated). Default: all three.
"project_status" — In Progress, Closed, Cancelled, On Hold, Admin Closure.
"state" — Active, Inactive.
"health" — Green/On Track, Yellow/At Risk, Red/Off Track.

list_statuses.description: If True, return counts grouped by group_by dimensions.

mode.description: "compact" (default) or "raw". Raw returns all Dataverse fields as JSON.

name.description: Person's display name. Defaults to logged-in user. Ignored when scope="org".

output_format.description: "md" (default) or "json". Ignored when mode="raw".

role.description:
How the person relates (scope="mine" only):
   "pm" (default), "team", "any", or D365 role name(s).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__create_sharepoint_folder
function.description:
Create a new folder in SharePoint or OneDrive.

Args:
    drive_id: Target drive ID (from search results or resolve_url).
    folder_name: Name for the new folder.
    parent_path: Parent folder path relative to drive root
        (e.g. "Documents/Reports"). Defaults to root.

Returns:
    Dictionary with id, name, web_url, full_path, and created date.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__create_teams_chat
function.description:
Create a new Teams chat with the given members.

The authenticated user is automatically included. For 1 other user,
creates a oneOnOne chat. For 2+ other users, creates a group chat.

Args:
    members: Email addresses of other participants (not including self).
    topic: Optional chat topic (group chats only; ignored for oneOnOne).

Returns:
    Dict with chat_id, chat_type, topic, created_by, members, web_url.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__download_teams_attachment
function.description:
Download file attachments and inline images from a Teams chat message.

Handles two storage backends transparently:
- File attachments (docs, PDFs, zips) are stored in SharePoint/OneDrive.
- Inline pasted images/screenshots are stored as hosted content on the
  message and downloaded directly from the Graph API.

**Recommended workflow:** Use ``mode="download_url"`` for file
attachments — this returns a short-lived SharePoint download link
instead of pushing file bytes through the conversation. Inline images
always return base64 regardless of mode (they have no SharePoint URL).

Use read_teams_message or poll_teams_messages first to discover
attachments (look for items with ``downloadable: true``).

Args:
    chat_id: Teams chat ID.
    message_id: Message ID containing the attachment(s).
    attachment_id: Download only this specific attachment. If omitted,
        downloads all downloadable attachments on the message.
    mode: "download_url" (recommended) returns a pre-authenticated
        SharePoint URL for file attachments — avoids sending large
        file content through the LLM context. "base64" returns file
        content inline as base64. Inline images always return base64.

Returns:
    Dict with chat_id, message_id, attachments (with content/URL),
    and skipped (non-downloadable items like adaptive cards).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__download_transcript_content
function.description:
Download the actual transcript content from a transcript URL.

Args:
    transcript_url: The transcript content URL (from the transcriptContentUrl field).

Returns:
    Dictionary with transcript content in VTT format.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_email_attachments
function.description:
Get attachments for a specific email message as short-lived download URLs.

Each attachment is uploaded to Azure Blob Storage and a pre-authenticated
SAS download URL is returned, valid for 5 minutes. Blobs are automatically
cleaned up by the container's lifecycle policy.

Repeated calls for the same attachment overwrite the same blob
(path: ``{message_id}/{attachment_id}/{filename}``), so no duplicates
accumulate in storage.

By default, inline attachments (signature images, embedded screenshots)
are excluded. Set include_inline=True to include them.

For OneDrive/SharePoint linked files embedded in the email body,
use read_email to discover the URLs (in the linked_files field),
then resolve_sharepoint_url + read_sharepoint_file to fetch content.

Args:
    message_id: The Graph API message ID.
    include_inline: Include inline attachments (default False).

Returns:
    List of attachment dictionaries with id, name, content_type,
    size, download_url (valid 5 min), is_inline, and content_id.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_email_attachments_base64
function.description:
Get attachments for a specific email message as base64-encoded content.

Returns raw attachment content as base64 in the response body. For large
attachments prefer get_email_attachments which uploads to blob storage
and returns a short-lived download URL instead.

By default, inline attachments (signature images, embedded
screenshots) are excluded. Set include_inline=True to include them.

Args:
    message_id: The Graph API message ID.
    include_inline: Include inline attachments (default False).

Returns:
    List of attachment dictionaries with id, name, content_type,
    size, and content_bytes (base64).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_meeting_attendees
function.description:
Get the list of attendees for a specific meeting.

Args:
    meeting_id: The calendar event ID.

Returns:
    Dictionary with organizer, invited attendees, actual attendees
    (from attendance reports), and attendance summary.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_my_info
function.description:
Get the authenticated user's Microsoft 365 profile.

Returns the caller's profile information from Microsoft Graph,
including display name, email, job title, and office location.

Returns:
    Dictionary with the user's profile fields from Microsoft Graph /me endpoint.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_recorded_meetings
function.description:
Get finished Teams meetings that have downloadable transcripts.

Returns only meetings with transcripts available. Use the transcriptContentUrl
from each transcript object with download_transcript_content to get the content.

Args:
    start_date: Start date in ISO format (e.g., "2024-01-01" or "2024-01-01T00:00:00").
    end_date: End date in ISO format (e.g., "2024-01-31" or "2024-01-31T23:59:59").
    subject_keywords: Optional comma-separated keywords to filter by subject (e.g., "standup,planning").

Returns:
    Dictionary with meeting count and list of meeting objects with transcript metadata.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_sharepoint_download_link
function.description:
Get a short-lived direct download URL for a SharePoint or OneDrive file.

Returns a pre-authenticated download link that can be opened in a browser
or used to download the file without further authentication (e.g. curl). The link
expires after a short period (typically a few minutes).

This is useful when you need to provide a clickable download link rather
than reading the file content directly.

Args:
    url: The full SharePoint or OneDrive URL of the file, as you would
        copy from a browser address bar. Supported formats include:
        - Site documents: https://{tenant}.sharepoint.com/sites/{site}/Shared Documents/{path}/{file}
        - Root documents: https://{tenant}.sharepoint.com/Shared Documents/{path}/{file}
        - OneDrive personal: https://{tenant}-my.sharepoint.com/personal/{user}/Documents/{file}
        Example: "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/Shared Documents/Reports/Q1_report.pdf"
    version_id: Optional version ID (from list_sharepoint_versions).
        If omitted, returns the download link for the current version.

Returns:
    Dictionary with download_url (the short-lived link), name, size,
    content_type, last_modified, version_id, drive_id, and item_id.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_sharepoint_upload_link
function.description:
Generate a short-lived public upload link for a SharePoint folder.

Creates a temporary URL that allows uploading files to the specified
SharePoint folder via a simple HTTP PUT request. The link is valid for
5 minutes and locked to the specified folder.

**Security:** The link expires after 5 minutes. The destination folder
is locked at creation time and cannot be changed at upload time. The
``file_limit`` parameter controls how many files can be uploaded
through the link (default 1). Once the limit is reached, further
uploads are rejected with a 403 error.

**How to use the returned upload URL:**

Send a PUT request with the file bytes as the body::

    PUT {upload_url}?file_name=report.pdf
    Content-Type: application/octet-stream

    <file bytes>

The ``file_name`` query parameter sets the file name in SharePoint.
Maximum file size is 100 MB per file.

**Example using curl:**

    curl -X PUT "{upload_url}?file_name=report.pdf" \
         -H "Content-Type: application/octet-stream" \
         --data-binary @report.pdf

Args:
    folder_url: The full SharePoint or OneDrive URL of the target folder.
        This is the folder where uploaded files will be placed. Supported
        formats include:
        - Site folders: https://{tenant}.sharepoint.com/sites/{site}/Shared Documents/{folder}
        - Root folders: https://{tenant}.sharepoint.com/Shared Documents/{folder}
        - OneDrive folders: https://{tenant}-my.sharepoint.com/personal/{user}/Documents/{folder}
        Example: "https://contoso.sharepoint.com/Shared Documents/Reports"
    file_limit: Maximum number of files that can be uploaded through
        this link (default 1). Once the limit is reached, further
        uploads are rejected.

Returns:
    Dictionary with upload_url, folder_url, folder_path, drive_id,
    file_limit, expires_at, and usage instructions.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_sharepoint_version_details
function.description:
Get detailed metadata for a specific file version.

Returns version size, modification date, and who modified it
(including email address when available).

Args:
    url: SharePoint or OneDrive file URL.
    version_id: Version ID from list_sharepoint_versions.

Returns:
    Dictionary with version_id, size, last_modified,
    modified_by, modified_by_email, drive_id, and item_id.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_user_automatic_replies
function.description:
Get a user's automatic replies (out-of-office) settings.

Returns OOO status, schedule, and reply messages (HTML stripped).
Uses app-level permissions to read any user's settings.

Args:
    user_id_or_email: User GUID or email address.

Returns:
    Dict with status ("disabled", "alwaysEnabled", "scheduled"),
    scheduled_start_datetime, scheduled_end_datetime,
    internal_reply_message, and external_reply_message.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_user_presence
function.description:
Get a user's Teams presence (activity and availability).

Accepts either a user GUID or email address.

Args:
    user_id_or_email: User GUID or email address.

Returns:
    Dict with activity, availability, and status_message.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__get_user_working_hours
function.description:
Get a user's working hours, timezone, and current local time.

Uses app-level (service principal) permissions to read any user's
mailbox settings. Requires MailboxSettings.Read application permission.

Args:
    user_id_or_email: User GUID or email address.

Returns:
    Dict with time_zone, time_zone_iana, days_of_week, start_time,
    end_time, and current_local_time. Returns error if not configured.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__list_sharepoint_folder
function.description:
List the contents of a SharePoint or OneDrive folder.

Browse files and subfolders in a specific folder. You can
identify the folder by item_id (from search results) or by
path. If both are provided, item_id takes precedence.
Defaults to the drive root.

Args:
    drive_id: The SharePoint/OneDrive drive ID.
    folder_path: Folder path relative to drive root
        (e.g. "Documents/Reports"). Defaults to root.
    item_id: Folder item ID (takes precedence over folder_path).

Returns:
    List of item dictionaries sorted folders-first, each with
    id, name, type ("file" or "folder"), web_url, last_modified,
    and size (files) or child_count (folders).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__list_sharepoint_versions
function.description:
List all versions of a SharePoint or OneDrive file.

Returns the current file metadata and a list of all previous
versions with their modification dates and authors.

Args:
    url: SharePoint or OneDrive file URL.

Returns:
    Dictionary with current_file metadata, versions list,
    total_versions count, drive_id, and item_id.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__list_teams_chats
function.description:
List Teams chats the authenticated user is part of.

Supports filtering by chat type, topic, and member name/email.
Results are sorted by most recent activity.

Args:
    chat_type: Filter by type — "oneOnOne", "group", or "meeting".
    topic: Case-insensitive substring match on chat topic.
    member: Case-insensitive match on member display name or email
            (automatically expands members).
    expand_members: Include member list for each chat.
    expand_last_message: Include last message preview.
    limit: Max chats per page (default 20, max 50).
    fetch_all: Follow pagination to fetch all chats.

Returns:
    List of chat dicts with chat_id, chat_type, topic, timestamps,
    and optionally members and last_message.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__lookup_user
function.description:
Look up a user's profile by email address.

Returns profile information including display name, job title,
department, office location, and phone numbers. Flags any
incomplete profile fields with an explanation.

Args:
    email: User's email address or User Principal Name.
    include_manager: If True, include the user's manager profile
        (1 extra API call). Default: False.

Returns:
    User profile dict with id, display_name, email, job_title, etc.
    Includes incomplete_fields list when profile data is missing.
    When include_manager=True, includes a manager key with the
    manager's profile or null if no manager is assigned.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__poll_teams_messages
function.description:
Retrieve messages from a Teams chat, optionally filtered by time.

Args:
    chat_id: Teams chat ID.
    since: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return messages created after this time.
    limit: Maximum number of messages to return (default 50).

Returns:
    List of message dicts sorted oldest-first with sender, content, and timestamp.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__read_calendar_event
function.description:
Read the full details of a specific calendar event.

Returns complete event data including body content, attendee RSVP
statuses with timestamps, attachments, series info, and all metadata.

Args:
    event_id: The Graph API calendar event ID.

Returns:
    Full calendar event dictionary with body, attendees (with RSVP details),
    location, recurrence, attachments, and all metadata fields.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__read_email
function.description:
Read the full content of a specific email.

Returns the complete email including body, recipients, attachments
metadata, and other fields.

Args:
    message_id: The Graph API message ID.

Returns:
    Full email dictionary with body, recipients, and metadata.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__read_sharepoint_file
function.description:
Read the content of a SharePoint or OneDrive file.

For files, downloads and returns content. For folders, returns
the list of children. Oversized files return metadata only
with a warning and web_url link.

Args:
    drive_id: The SharePoint/OneDrive drive ID (from search results).
    item_id: The file or folder item ID (from search results).
    mode: Content mode — "text" (default) extracts readable text
        from Office formats (docx, pptx, xlsx, pdf). "markdown"
        preserves document structure (headings, bullets, tables)
        as markdown — ideal for converting between formats (e.g.
        read a docx as markdown, then upload as pdf/pptx).
        "base64" returns raw base64-encoded bytes.
    max_size_mb: Maximum file size in MB to download (default 10,
        hard cap 100). Files exceeding this return metadata with
        a warning and web_url link.

Returns:
    Dictionary with file metadata, content, and encoding. For
    folders, includes a children list instead of content.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__read_sharepoint_page
function.description:
Read the content of a SharePoint site page.

Returns the page title, description, URL, and HTML content
extracted from the page's canvas layout.

Args:
    site_id: The SharePoint site ID.
    page_id: The SharePoint UniqueId GUID for the page
        (e.g. "a287074e-41b2-4ae5-8a3b-6475cb9ce588").
        This is NOT the drive item_id. Use the unique_id field
        returned by resolve_sharepoint_url, or the unique_id
        from list_sharepoint_folder.

Returns:
    Dictionary with page id, title, description, web_url,
    created, last_modified, and content_html.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__read_sharepoint_site
function.description:
Read SharePoint site information.

Returns metadata about a SharePoint site including its name,
description, URL, and dates.

Args:
    site_id: The SharePoint site ID.

Returns:
    Dictionary with site id, name, description, web_url,
    created, and last_modified.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__read_teams_message
function.description:
Read a single Teams chat message by chat ID and message ID.

Use this after search_teams_messages or poll_teams_messages to get full
message detail including mentions, attachments, threading, and edit history.

Args:
    chat_id: Teams chat ID.
    message_id: Message ID.

Returns:
    Full normalized message dict with all enriched fields.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__resolve_sharepoint_url
function.description:
Resolve a SharePoint or OneDrive URL to Graph API identifiers.

Accepts any browser-copyable SharePoint or OneDrive URL and returns
the drive_id and item_id needed for other SharePoint tools.

Args:
    url: SharePoint or OneDrive file/folder URL.

Returns:
    Dictionary with drive_id, item_id, unique_id, site_id, name,
    web_url, folder_path, size, last_modified, and is_folder.
    Pass unique_id + site_id directly to read_sharepoint_page.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__search_calendar_events
function.description:
Search calendar events with flexible filters.

All filters can be freely combined (AND logic). Text matching is
case-insensitive substring against subject, body preview, and location.

Use query='*' or omit to browse all events in the date range.
When combining broad queries with tight filters, results may be
fewer than limit due to client-side filtering.

Args:
    query: Text to search in subject, body, or location. Use '*' or omit for all events.
    after_datetime: Events starting after this date (ISO 8601). Default: 30 days ago.
    before_datetime: Events ending before this date (ISO 8601). Default: 30 days from now.
    attendee: Filter by attendee email (partial match, any TO/CC attendee).
    organizer: Filter by organizer email (partial match).
    calendar_name: Restrict search to a specific calendar by exact name (e.g. "Personal", "Work").
    limit: Maximum results (default 25, max 5000).
    offset: Results to skip for pagination (default 0, max 5000).
    check_transcripts: If True, check each Teams meeting for transcripts
        (2 extra API calls per unique meeting). Default: False.

Returns:
    Dictionary with count and list of calendar event objects including
    rich metadata (showAs, isAllDay, isCancelled, recurrence, categories, etc.)
    plus Teams join URL, calendar_id/calendar_name, and optional transcript data.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__search_emails
function.description:
Search emails in the authenticated user's mailbox.

All filters can be freely combined. Text search params (sender,
recipient, subject, query) use Microsoft Graph KQL search. When
text search is active, structured filters (dates, is_read,
importance) are applied client-side after fetching results.

When combining broad text searches with tight date filters,
results may be fewer than ``limit`` because client-side filtering
trims the over-fetched set.

Args:
    sender: Filter by sender email address (full or partial).
    recipient: Filter by recipient email (matches TO, CC, or BCC).
    subject: Filter where subject contains this string.
    subject_startswith: Filter where subject starts with this string.
    subject_exact: Filter where subject exactly matches this string.
    query: Free-text search across subject, body, and attachments.
    folder: Folder name to search in (e.g. "Inbox", "Sent Items").
    after_date: Emails received after this date (ISO 8601).
    before_date: Emails received before this date (ISO 8601).
    has_attachments: Filter by attachment presence. Note: Graph API
        does not count inline images as attachments, so emails with
        only inline images will have has_attachments=False.
    is_read: Filter by read status.
    importance: Filter by importance ("low", "normal", "high").
    limit: Maximum number of results (default 25, max 5000).
    offset: Number of results to skip for pagination (default 0,
        max 5000). Use with limit to page through results.

Returns:
    List of email summary dictionaries.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__search_meetings_by_name
function.description:
Search for meetings by name/subject using case-insensitive substring matching.

Args:
    search_term: The search term to match against meeting subjects.
    start_date: Start date in ISO format (e.g., "2024-01-01").
    end_date: End date in ISO format (e.g., "2024-01-31").
    check_transcripts: Optional. If True, include has_transcript field. Default: False.

Returns:
    Dictionary with count and list of matching meetings.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__search_sharepoint_pages
function.description:
Search SharePoint site pages by filename, author, or date.

Lists pages on a SharePoint site with server-side filtering.
Pass any URL on the target site — the tool extracts the site
automatically (no resolve_sharepoint_url call needed).

All filters are server-side to minimise token usage. Use
name_contains for keyword search (matches the .aspx filename,
which is a hyphenated version of the page title). Use
name_range_start/end for alphabetical paging on large sites.

**Recommended workflow:** Search with metadata only (default),
hand the user ``web_url`` links. Use ``include_content="text"``
for lightweight content preview. Use ``read_sharepoint_page``
with the returned ``site_id`` and page ``id`` for full content.

Args:
    site_url: Any URL on the target SharePoint site (e.g.
        "https://waterfield365.sharepoint.com/sites/Intranet").
    name_contains: Substring match on filename (e.g. "Holiday"
        matches "Holiday-Calenders.aspx").
    name_prefix: Prefix match on filename (e.g. "Ho" matches
        "Holiday-Calenders.aspx" and "Home.aspx").
    name_range_start: Alphabetical lower bound (e.g. "A" for
        pages starting A onwards). Combine with name_range_end.
    name_range_end: Alphabetical upper bound (e.g. "F" for
        pages before F). Use "Z{" for all through Z.
    author: Filter by last modified author. Full name uses
        exact match; single word uses partial match.
    after_date: Pages modified after this date (ISO 8601).
    before_date: Pages modified before this date (ISO 8601).
    include_content: "none" (default) returns metadata only.
        "text" returns page content as plain text (lightweight).
        "html" returns raw HTML from canvasLayout.
    limit: Maximum results (default 25, max 50).

Returns:
    Dictionary with pages list, count, total, site_id,
    site_url, has_more. Each page has id, title, name,
    web_url, description, created, last_modified, created_by,
    last_modified_by, and optionally content.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__search_sharepoint_sites
function.description:
Search SharePoint sites the authenticated user can access.

Returns sites matching a keyword. Use ``query="*"`` (the default)
to list all sites the user has permission to access. This is the
recommended first step when the user asks about SharePoint content
and you don't know which site to target.

The returned ``id`` can be passed to ``read_sharepoint_site`` for
full metadata, or to ``read_sharepoint_page`` / ``search_sharepoint_pages``
(via site_url from ``web_url``) to work with site pages.

Args:
    query: Search keyword to match site name or content.
        Use "*" (default) to list all accessible sites.
    limit: Maximum results (default 25, max 50).

Returns:
    Dictionary with sites list (id, name, description, web_url,
    created, last_modified), count, and query.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__search_teams_messages
function.description:
Search Teams chat messages across all chats using keyword or KQL syntax.

Args:
    query: Free-text or KQL query. Supports from:, sent:, hasAttachment: operators.
    after_datetime: ISO 8601 — only messages on or after this date.
    before_datetime: ISO 8601 — only messages on or before this date.
    sender: Filter by sender email (e.g. alice@example.com).
    limit: Max results to return (default 25, max 100).
    offset: Skip first N results for pagination (default 0).

Returns:
    Dict with count, more_results, and messages list (message_id, chat_id,
    sender, body, timestamps, importance, rank, summary).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__send_email
function.description:
Send an email as the authenticated user.

Args:
    to_email: Recipient email address.
    subject: Email subject line.
    body: Email body content (HTML or plain text).
    content_type: Body content type — "HTML" (default) or "Text".

Returns:
    Status dictionary confirming the email was sent.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__send_teams_message
function.description:
Send a text message to a Teams chat.

Args:
    chat_id: Teams chat ID.
    message: Message text to send.
    content_type: "text" or "html". Defaults to "text".

Returns:
    The Graph API message response (id, createdDateTime, webUrl, etc.).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__send_teams_message_and_wait
function.description:
Create a chat, send a message, and poll until a reply arrives.

Orchestrates the full send-and-wait workflow:
1. Create chat with members
2. Send the message
3. Poll for new messages until a reply or timeout

Args:
    members: Email addresses of other participants.
    message: Message text to send.
    content_type: "text" or "html". Defaults to "text".
    topic: Optional chat topic (group chats only).
    poll_interval_seconds: Seconds between poll attempts (default 30).
    max_wait_seconds: Maximum seconds to wait for a reply (default 600).

Returns:
    Dict with status ("reply_received" or "timeout"), chat_id,
    web_url, poll_count, elapsed_seconds, and replies (if any).

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__sharepoint_folder_search
function.description:
Search for folders across SharePoint and OneDrive by name.

Finds folders matching the query across all sites and drives
the authenticated user has access to.

Args:
    query: Folder name to search for (partial match).
    limit: Maximum number of results (default 25, max 50).

Returns:
    List of folder dictionaries with id, drive_id, name,
    web_url, and last_modified. Use drive_id and id with
    list_sharepoint_folder to browse contents.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__sharepoint_search
function.description:
Search SharePoint and OneDrive documents by content.

Searches across all SharePoint sites and OneDrive files the
authenticated user has access to. Returns matching documents
with metadata and content snippets.

All filters can be freely combined to narrow results.

When fallback=False (default), performs a single fast search and
returns a flat list of results. When fallback=True, automatically
retries with alternative query strategies if the primary search
returns no results (lowercase, wildcard, first-two-words), and
includes disambiguation info for duplicate file names.

Args:
    query: Free-text search query. Searches document content,
        filenames, and metadata.
    file_type: Filter by file extension (e.g. "pdf", "docx",
        "xlsx", "pptx").
    author: Filter by author name or email (partial match).
    folder_name: Filter by parent folder name (partial match).
    after_date: Only include documents modified after this date
        (ISO 8601, e.g. "2026-01-01").
    before_date: Only include documents modified before this date
        (ISO 8601, e.g. "2026-03-01").
    min_size_mb: Only include files at least this size in MB
        (e.g. 5.0 for files >= 5 MB). Applied client-side, so
        fewer results than limit may be returned.
    max_size_mb: Only include files at most this size in MB
        (e.g. 10.0 for files <= 10 MB). Applied client-side, so
        fewer results than limit may be returned.
    limit: Maximum number of results (default 25, max 50).
    offset: Number of results to skip for pagination (default 0,
        max 1000).
    fallback: Enable intelligent fallback strategies when the
        primary search returns no results (default False). When
        True, retries with lowercase, wildcard, and first-two-words
        queries, and returns disambiguation info. Uses 1-4 API
        calls instead of 1.

Returns:
    When fallback=False: list of document dicts with id, drive_id,
    name, web_url, summary, last_modified, and size. Use drive_id
    and id with read_sharepoint… [truncated]

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_Waterfield_M365__sharepoint_url_to_path
function.description:
Parse a SharePoint URL into site, folder path, and file name.

Performs local URL parsing only — no API call required. Useful for
quickly extracting path components from SharePoint URLs.

Args:
    url: SharePoint or OneDrive URL.

Returns:
    Dictionary with site, folder_path, file_name, full_path,
    and original_url.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_WTI_Monitoring_Staging__authenticate
function.description: The `claude.ai WTI Monitoring Staging` MCP server (claudeai-proxy at https://a1b-mcp-infosec-staging1-hzd2d8bkfka9fyf0.z03.azurefd.net/mcp) is installed but requires authentication. Call this tool to start the OAuth flow — you'll receive an authorization URL to share with the user. Once the user completes authorization in their browser, the server's real tools will become available automatically.

function.name: mcp__claude_ai_WTI_Monitoring_Staging__complete_authentication
function.description: Complete an in-progress OAuth flow for the `claude.ai WTI Monitoring Staging` MCP server by submitting the callback URL. Call `mcp__claude_ai_WTI_Monitoring_Staging__authenticate` first to start the flow and get the authorization URL. After the user authorizes in their browser, the browser is redirected to a `http://localhost:<port>/callback?code=...&state=...` URL — on remote sessions that page fails to load, but the URL in the address bar is still valid. Pass that full URL here as `callback_url`.

callback_url.description: The full callback URL from the browser address bar after authorizing, e.g. http://localhost:<port>/callback?code=...&state=...

function.name: mcp__plugin_context7_context7__query-docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best information you have.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp__plugin_context7_context7__resolve-library-id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.
